finding: check_docs_index has no adopter/framework guard (unlike check_state_template_sync) and FAILs if any docs/**/*.md is unlinked from README. Lightweight install projects docs/spec/{prd,epic,task}-template.md into the adopter's docs/, which their README won't link -> the documented 'verify install' gate command fails immediately.
recommendation: Add the skill/hedl-at-root guard (return None for adopters) or scope in_scope to framework-only paths; document framework-only checks.

finding: tiers.md Tier-2 says copy skill/hedl into .claude/skills/Hedl/ and copy .work/, but install.py/tiers.json never create .claude/skills/ — they project individual files. Newcomer builds a layout the tool never references.
recommendation: Rewrite Tier-2 manual block to match the projection model; point to install.py --tier lightweight as the single path.

finding: SKILL.md routes install/doctor/status/migrate/version to skill/hedl/scripts/install.py — the FRAMEWORK layout. In an adopter repo the skill lives under .claude/skills/hedl/, so that path is 'No such file or directory'. (This is WORK-0001.)
recommendation: Project install.py to a stable adopter path or make SKILL.md paths adopter-resolvable. Tracked as WORK-0001.

finding: 'tier' means both adoption tier (install.py --tier) and budget/review tier (budget_manager tier); both in SKILL.md routing and README:75 'when the tier drops' sits next to adoption-tier prose.
recommendation: Rename one axis ('review budget level' vs 'adoption tier') everywhere or add a Vocabulary entry.

finding: Middle tier shown as Lightweight / lightweight / 'Lightweight (recommended)'; brief used 'solo' which exists nowhere. Prose names not always adjacent to --tier CLI values.
recommendation: One display name per tier, always next to literal --tier value; purge any 'solo'.

finding: gate-only curl fetches only am_i_done.py; sibling checks (pr-template, markdown-schema, budget, skill-meta) silently no-op when missing, so the curl gate is strictly weaker than install.py --tier gate and docs don't say so.
recommendation: curl the full gate set, or use install.py --tier gate, or list which checks are unavailable.

finding: tiers.md says .claude/skills/Hedl/ (capital) vs am_i_done.py/getting-started lowercase 'hedl'; divergence on case-sensitive FS.
--migrate and --version absent from adopter docs though --doctor instructs running --migrate on stale schema.
recommendation: Add --migrate/--version and the needs-migrate recovery loop to getting-started/commands.md.

finding: commands.md Standard panel mixes named agent (security-auditor) with library prompts (new-engineer, quality-synthesizer); review-dispatcher counted in '8 agents' is a selector not a reviewer.
recommendation: Label reviewers by roster; clarify dispatcher is not a reviewer.
